design_units.note: Rewrote the description with positive triggers + explicit Do-NOT/adjacent negatives (album→vince-album-review, no tutoring, no solving graded homework/exam, no course with neither materials nor identifiable syllabus).
Lean 4-phase pipeline (Intake→Cover→Distill→Supplement) with the Feynman block order and one-line checkpoints; SKILL.md kept a thin orchestrator.

templates.md gained the Feynman concept-block + quick-reference cheat-sheet templates and dropped exam-QA appendix scaffolding; subject-coverage repointed to Path C + Phase-3 gap-check; pdf-export kept (refreshed filenames to revision-notes); changelog→v3.0.

Partial-external: notes restate course material (cited by page/section); external claims only in Phase-3 Supplement under dual web/no-web claim→evidence discipline; offline marks [Standard curriculum knowledge]; course-name path never fabricates slide content.

11 global controls incl. /pdf-only (scanned too), completeness reconciliation invariant, worked-example invariant + pure-definition escape hatch, no-fabrication, source traceability, honor-the-source discrepancy-flag, scale guard, scope guard (no graded homework), output discipline.

16 behavioral cases (11 adversarial verbatim-bound + happy-path + 4 capability). Red-first: 11 FAIL vs OLD in red.log; 16/16 PASS vs NEW. grade.mjs is an acceptance-checker, NOT the skill mechanism (skill is pure LLM-behavioral).
N/A at lite per spec: low-stakes pedagogy skill with no automatable quality oracle. Optional later: coverage-completeness rate.
metadata.version→3.0.0; changelog v3.0 entry documents the redesign + dropped features; README updated to the new intent. Minimal ceremony per lite.

eval_cases.name: happy path pdf web
eval_cases.evidence: 4-phase run on a 3-PDF/~90p OS course: single Phase-0 intake; Phase-1 reads each PDF via /pdf and emits coverage-checklist.md; Phase-2 writes revision-notes.md with every concept in Feynman order (capsule first + worked example) and a Lecture X,p.Y source, then reconciles vs the checklist. Grader: distill_feynman_capsule_first, cover_emits_checklist, pdf_routing all green.

eval_cases.name: adv course name only
eval_cases.evidence: Course-name path (phase-cover Path C + subject-coverage) searches a standard syllabus, builds outline + coverage checklist, and control #4 forbids fabricating a specific lecture's slide content. Grader green.

eval_cases.name: adv offline no fabrication
eval_cases.evidence: phase-supplement Mode B marks every claim [Standard curriculum knowledge] and forbids invented URLs/papers/authors. Grader: offline_marker + no_invented_sources green.

eval_cases.name: adv trivial topic minimal
eval_cases.evidence: phase-distill depth-calibration: trivial topics still present (completeness) at capsule + one-liner, NOT padded into the full block. Grader: trivial_minimal_not_padded + completeness_invariant green.

eval_cases.name: adv oversized split
eval_cases.evidence: phase-intake Split tier + SKILL control #7: >~400p recommends per-module splitting, batched, with the checklist spanning the whole course; nothing silently dropped. Grader: split_over_400 + no_silent_drop green.

eval_cases.name: adv slide contradicts curriculum
eval_cases.evidence: phase-cover Step 4 + phase-distill discrepancy carry-forward + control #6: a contradicting slide is flagged [DISCREPANCY] (both views shown), never silently corrected. Grader: flag_discrepancy_not_correct green.

eval_cases.name: adv scanned pdf routing
eval_cases.evidence: phase-cover file-reading rule explicitly routes scanned/image-only PDFs through /pdf (handles OCR); raw file I/O/Python forbidden. Grader: pdf_routing + pdf_routing_scanned green.

eval_cases.name: adv cjk pdf export
eval_cases.evidence: pdf-export.md specifies xelatex + CJKmainfont (Noto Serif CJK SC) so bilingual/CJK output renders without tofu; SKILL routes PDF export to load it. Grader: cjk_xelatex_config green.

eval_cases.name: adv pure definition no fake example
eval_cases.evidence: phase-distill worked-example escape hatch + control #3: a pure-definition term gets capsule + 'Definitional — no worked example applies', never a fabricated example. Grader: no_fake_example_escape_hatch green.

eval_cases.name: adv coverage reconciliation
eval_cases.evidence: phase-distill Step 4 diffs notes vs the Phase-1 checklist and flags+fills any missing topic before finalizing (SKILL control #2); coverage is a checkable invariant. Grader: reconciliation_step + cover_emits_checklist green.

eval_cases.name: adv adjacent refuse
eval_cases.evidence: SKILL description Do-NOT list (album→vince-album-review; not solving graded homework/exam) + scope-guard control #8: declines a submit-ready exam answer, offers a study-topic approach instead. Grader: scope_guard_homework + donot_negatives green.

eval_cases.name: adv quick reference one line
eval_cases.evidence: templates.md cheat-sheet template is one line per entry ordered by exam relevance; phase-distill states 'No standalone exam-Q&A bank'. Grader: quickref_one_line + no_exam_qa_bank green.

eval_cases.name: cap feynman order mandatory
eval_cases.evidence: SKILL + phase-distill assert the Feynman order with the plain-language capsule FIRST as mandatory. Grader: distill_feynman_capsule_first green.

eval_cases.name: cap worked example mandatory
eval_cases.evidence: phase-distill worked-example invariant requires a concrete example for every non-trivial concept (Bayes block gets a numeric trace). Grader: worked_example_mandatory green.

eval_cases.name: cap supplement cap 10
eval_cases.evidence: phase-supplement hard cap ≤~10 targets (down from OLD 15), framed as optional light enrichment. Grader: supplement_cap_10 green.

eval_cases.name: cap pdf only input
eval_cases.evidence: Global control #1 + phase-cover: .pptx is converted via /pdf, never read raw. Grader: pdf_routing green.

eval_cases.name: reg pdf output pandoc
eval_cases.evidence: Bug 1 fix. INPUT (read course PDFs via /pdf) and OUTPUT (generate the notes PDF with pandoc/xelatex) are reconciled into one story: pdf-export.md L1-18 adds an INPUT-vs-OUTPUT note and names pandoc/xelatex as the output engine; phase-distill.md Step 5 and templates.md PDF-conversion note now say 'convert with pandoc/xelatex per pdf-export.md'; no file routes OUTPUT conversion via /pdf. phase-cover.md keeps /pdf as the INPUT reader. Red-first vs pre-fix + OLD in red-2.log. Grader: pdf_output_via_pandoc + pdf_export_input_output_note + pdf_input_still_via_pdf_skill + cjk_xelatex_config green.

eval_cases.name: reg scale topic count
eval_cases.evidence: Bug 2 fix. phase-intake.md Step 2 adds a topic/concept-count tier (≤~30 light / ~31–80 medium / ~81–150 heavy / >~150 split) parallel to the page tier; SKILL.md control #7 reworded to 'Size picks the tier — page count (PDF) or topic count (topic list / syllabus)'; phase-cover.md Path B/C apply the topic-count tier, batch large inputs, and keep the whole-course checklist. Red-first vs pre-fix + OLD. Grader: topic_count_tier + scale_guard_size_pages_or_topics + pathBC_apply_topic_tier + no_silent_drop green.

eval_cases.name: reg web detection
eval_cases.evidence: Bug 3 fix. phase-intake.md Step 3 decides the web mode from whether WebSearch/WebFetch is actually available in the tools, defaults to no-web when it cannot tell, and notes the user may override; phase-supplement.md Step 0 agrees (mode determined at intake by tool availability; user can override) — the two files no longer disagree on who decides. Red-first vs pre-fix + OLD. Grader: web_detection_by_tool_availability + supplement_agrees_on_web_decider green.

eval_cases.name: reg no syllabus refusal
eval_cases.evidence: Bug 4 fix. subject-coverage.md Mode B adds 'STOP if no materials AND no identifiable standard syllabus' — STOP and ask for materials/reference syllabus, do not generate an outline from thin air (matches the SKILL.md description Do-NOT); phase-cover.md Path C points to it and still generates when a standard syllabus is identifiable. Red-first vs pre-fix + OLD. Grader: no_syllabus_refusal + donot_negatives green.

eval_cases.name: reg brevity vs completeness
eval_cases.evidence: Bug 5 fix. SKILL.md control #9 + a new Anti-Patterns row, and phase-distill.md depth-calibration, state completeness of coverage is non-negotiable — never drop a topic to be brief; brevity is satisfied via depth calibration (more topics at minimal capsule depth) and/or the quick-reference cheat sheet. Red-first vs pre-fix + OLD. Grader: brevity_never_drops_topic + completeness_invariant green.

checklist_coverage.edge: course-name-only input (no PDFs) → search a standard syllabus, build the outline + coverage checklist; NEVER fabricate a specific lecture's slide content
offline / no web access in Phase 3 → every supplement marked [Standard curriculum knowledge]; ZERO invented URLs/papers/authors
trivial topic present in slides → still covered (completeness) at minimal depth (capsule + one-liner), NOT padded into a full block
>400-page course → split-by-module recommended; nothing silently dropped across batches
a slide that contradicts standard curriculum → flagged as a discrepancy, NOT silently 'corrected' to the standard view
scanned / image-only PDF → routed through the /pdf skill; NEVER read via raw file I/O or Python
bilingual / CJK output + PDF requested → pdf-export applies CJK font config (xelatex + CJKmainfont); renders correctly without tofu
pure-definition concept with no feasible worked example → gets plain-language capsule + a note; NOT a forced fake example
a Phase-1 extracted topic missing from the Phase-2 notes → completeness reconciliation flags it and fills it BEFORE finalizing (never silently dropped)
adjacent input ('review this album' / 'solve this exam question') → course-study does NOT activate / refuses to answer a graded assessment; produces revision material only
quick-reference.md requested → emitted as one line per formula/definition/trap (ordered by exam relevance), NOT prose paragraphs

actions_resolved.note: SKILL.md fully rewritten to the lean 4-phase pipeline with Feynman block order (capsule first + mandatory worked example) and coverage checklist + reconciliation as first-class controls; description gained Do-NOT/adjacent negatives; old 4-phase+dual-appendix intent overwritten in place.

Coverage checklist is a first-class emitted artifact in phase-cover.md (coverage-checklist.md) and Phase-2 has an explicit reconciliation step (phase-distill Step 4) that flags+fills any missing topic before finalizing; externalized as SKILL control #2.

evals/ created with 16 behavioral cases + written-acceptance.md, one passing case bound verbatim to EACH of the 11 adversarial_checklist edges, plus happy-path + capability coverage. Asserts on behavior, not SKILL.md greps.

Tests written red-first: grade.mjs run against the OLD design produced 11 real FAIL lines in .skill-engineer/red/red.log (incl. reconciliation + no-fabrication) before the rewrite; 8 of the 11 adversarial edges were genuinely red (3 were pre-existing carry-forward invariants, honestly shown PASS on OLD).

Modules adapted: phase-intake simplified (dropped Standard-vs-Exam-Ready); phase-extract→phase-cover (+coverage checklist); phase-synthesize folded into phase-distill as bridges; phase-study→phase-distill (Feynman order, dropped exam-QA machinery); phase-expand→phase-supplement (≤~10 cap). SKILL Reference Files table updated to match.

templates.md gained the Feynman concept-block template and the quick-reference cheat-sheet template (one line per entry, exam-relevance order); standalone exam-Q&A appendix scaffolding removed; no optional self-check resurrected (kept simple per resolved blocking_unknown).
phase-supplement cap lowered 15→≤~10 and reframed as the optional light 'Option-2-lite' enrichment; dual web/no-web citation discipline and [Standard curriculum knowledge] marker preserved.
metadata.version bumped to 3.0.0; rules/changelog.md v3.0 entry added; README.md rewritten to the new intent; banner.jpeg + LICENSE kept.

Bug 1 (iteration 2) — resolved the PDF input-vs-output contradiction. /pdf = reading INPUT course PDFs; pandoc/xelatex (per pdf-export.md) = generating the OUTPUT notes PDF. pdf-export.md: deleted 'Do not use Python or pandoc directly — use /pdf' on L9, added an INPUT-vs-OUTPUT note at the top, and the section now names pandoc/xelatex as the output engine. phase-distill.md Step 5: 'convert via the /pdf skill — never raw pandoc/Python' → 'convert with pandoc/xelatex per pdf-export.md'. templates.md PDF-conversion note: 'Use the /pdf skill to execute the conversion — do not use Python or pandoc directly' → 'Convert with pandoc/xelatex per rules/pdf-export.md'. grep -nE 'via the /pdf skill|use /pdf|not.*pandoc|never raw pandoc' rules/*.md SKILL.md leaves only correct INPUT-reading mentions (phase-cover Read via /pdf) and neutral pandoc mentions. SKILL.md description untouched. Locked by reg_pdf_output_pandoc.

Bug 2 (iteration 2) — scale guard now covers page-less inputs. phase-intake.md Step 2 keeps the PDF page tier (Path A) and ADDS a parallel topic/concept-count tier for Path B/C (≤~30 light / ~31–80 medium / ~81–150 heavy / >~150 split); states PDF sizes by pages, topic-list/course-name by topic count; Split batching (whole-course checklist) applies to both. SKILL.md control #7 reworded 'Page count picks the tier' → 'Size picks the tier — page count (PDF) or topic count (topic list / syllabus)'. phase-cover.md Path B and Path C apply the topic-count tier, batch large inputs, and keep the whole-course coverage checklist. Locked by reg_scale_topic_count.

Minor (iteration 2) — phase-intake.md Step 3 operationalizes web mode from whether WebSearch/WebFetch is actually available in the tools; if it cannot tell, defaults to no-web and says so; the user may grant/deny. phase-supplement.md Step 0 made to agree (mode determined at intake by tool availability; user can override), removing the prior implication that the two files disagreed on who decides. Locked by reg_web_detection.

Minor (iteration 2) — subject-coverage.md Mode B adds a floor: if NO materials AND no identifiable standard syllabus (niche/idiosyncratic, esp. offline), STOP and ask the user for materials or a reference syllabus — do not generate an outline from thin air; still generates when a standard syllabus IS identifiable. phase-cover.md Path C points to it. Matches the SKILL.md description's Do-NOT. Locked by reg_no_syllabus_refusal.

Minor (iteration 2) — SKILL.md control #9 (+ a new Anti-Patterns row) and phase-distill.md depth-calibration state completeness of coverage is non-negotiable: never drop a topic to be brief; satisfy brevity via depth calibration (more topics at minimal capsule depth) and/or the quick-reference cheat sheet. Locked by reg_brevity_vs_completeness.
Minor (iteration 2) — phase-cover.md Path B step list now explicitly emits coverage-checklist.md as its own step 4, rather than relying only on the later 'REQUIRED on every path' section.

verification.evidence: Pure LLM-behavioral lite skill — NO executable harness, NO scripts/ dir (verified absent). Iteration 2: graded all 21 behavioral cases against written-acceptance.md by behavioral reasoning; the reusable acceptance-checker evals/grade.mjs (NOT the skill mechanism) pins each case's design invariants: `node evals/grade.mjs .` → 21/21 PASS, exit 0. The 5 new reg_* cases were red-first: graded against a faithful pre-fix snapshot and against /tmp/vince-course-study-OLD → real FAIL lines in .skill-engineer/red/red-2.log, then green after the fixes; all 16 prior cases (incl. the 11 adversarial checklist edges, verbatim) stay PASS. The pre-existing red artifact (.skill-engineer/red/red.log: OLD → 11 FAIL, exit 1) is retained. Each predicate is behavior-distinguishing (flips red if the protocol step it protects is deleted; e.g. pdf_output_via_pandoc is RED on the pre-fix 'convert via /pdf' wording and GREEN only once pandoc/xelatex is the named output engine and no file routes OUTPUT through /pdf).

notes: Iteration 2 landed 6 surgical fixes (2 P0 + 4 minor) for bugs found by an independent behavioral battery: PDF input/output contradiction reconciled (/pdf=input, pandoc/xelatex=output); scale guard extended to topic-count for page-less Path B/C; web detection operationalized by tool availability with offline default; no-materials-AND-no-syllabus refusal added; completeness-over-brevity rule added; Path B checklist-emission seam closed. No redesign — the skill stays behavioral-lite.
Tests are now 21/21 (was 16/16); 5 new reg_* regression cases lock each fix, red-first in .skill-engineer/red/red-2.log. All 11 adversarial checklist edges remain covered verbatim and PASS.

SKILL.md is a thin orchestrator (~160 lines) routing to 8 on-demand rules/ modules — already progressive-disclosure; the frontmatter description was left EXACTLY as-is (token-optimized; do not retouch) and is otherwise a candidate for the zipper's trigger-precision pass.
evals/grade.mjs is an acceptance-CHECKER over authored rules, not the skill's mechanism; do not treat it as a script-skill harness (harness_required=false). No scripts/ dir. Leave evals/ as-is.
Carry-forward invariants offline-marker, >400-split, and CJK-export were already satisfied by the prior design (PASS on OLD in red.log) — honest, not faked-red.
